title: Eusteralis helferi
intro: Eusteralis helferi, also known as Eusteralis, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Rutaceae and primarily found in the forests of Myanmar (Burma) and Thailand. The Eusteralis plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimalarial effects, as well as being used to treat various other ailments including fever and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Eusteralis include flavonoids, limonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eusteralis is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, with the most common herbal preparations being used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
